name: Coronary Artery Disease
summary: Coronary artery disease reflects atherosclerotic narrowing of coronary arteries. GENARCH summarizes population-level genetic architecture and exposure modifiers for education only.
adolescent_relevance: CAD events occur later in life, but exposure and cardiometabolic trajectories begin earlier; GENARCH content is educational only.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS is discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not project individual risk.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Diet patterns influence LDL metabolism and inflammation.
Smoking is strongly associated with CAD burden in cohorts.
Ambient particulates are associated with cardiovascular outcomes in population studies.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Many large CAD GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS performance can vary across ancestries due to LD and cohort composition.
population_equity.data_gaps: Exposure measurement differs across settings; GxE evidence is incomplete.

references.title: Genome-wide association meta-analysis of coronary artery disease identifies loci and pathways
The Health Consequences of Smoking—50 Years of Progress
references.journal: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

references.title: Air pollution and health: evidence and mechanisms (review)
references.journal: Journal of the Air & Waste Management Association
